Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendant Fine Restaurants, Inc. To Provide Further Responses To Plaintiff'S 1st Set Of Form Interrogatories
Off Calendar Jan-13-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
William S. Weisberg, Esq., for Plaintiff and moving party: Shahrzad Saeed
Jenny Li, Esq., for Defendant and opposing party: John S. Dupen
AFTER HEARING, TENTATIVE IS RULING ADOPTED: Plaintiff's Motion To Compel Defendant Fine Restaurants, Inc. To Provide Further Responses To Plaintiff's First Set Of Form Interrogatories
The preliminary statement is stricken.
The motion is timely as it seeks further substantive responses. As to objections, the 45 days to bring a motion begins from the service of objections with the signature of the attorney. As to substantive portions of the response, they are not responses unless they are verified, a motion may be brought to obtain supplemental substantive responses.
The motion is denied as to 12.1 as the response, now verified, is that there are no witnesses.
The motion is granted as to 12.4 as approximately is evasive. Plaintiff has waived the right to have the objections overruled and ordered stricken.
The motion is granted to Numbers 15.1 as the motion seeks a further substantive response as the response is inadequate.
The motion is denied as to 16.2 and 50.1, 50.3, 50.4, 50.5, and 50.6 as the responses are adequate.
The motion is granted as to 50.2 as the response is inadequate.
Further responses shall be made within 15 days of notice of this order. Order was signed in open court. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendant Helen Economou To Provide Further Responses To Plaintiff To Plaintiff'S 1st Set Of Document Request And Product Documents
Off Calendar Jan-13-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
William S. Weisberg, Esq., for Plaintiff and moving party: Shahrzad Saeed
Jenny Li, Esq., for Defendant and opposing party: John S. Dupen

AFTER HEARING, TENTATIVE IS RULING ADOPTED: Plaintiff Shahrzad Saeed's Motion To Compel Defendant Helen Economou To Provide Further Responses To Plaintiff's First Set Of Document Requests And To Produce Documents is granted in part and denied in part.
The motion is timely as it seeks further substantive responses. As to objections, the 45 days to bring a motion begins from the service of objections with the signature of the attorney. As to substantive portions of the response, they are not responses unless they are verified, a motion may be brought to obtain supplemental substantive responses.
The motion is granted as to Numbers 16 and 17, the motion is granted as Defendant must identify documents with specificity. The motion is granted as to No. 20 as the declaration sheet is insufficient.
The motion is granted in part and denied in part as to Numbers 24, 46. The responses are adequate except as they refer to other documents produced in discovery without identifying them with specificity.
The motion is granted as to Numbers 27, 28, 29, 30, 31, 32, 33, 34, 35-45, and 47-81 as the identification of 'all pleadings and discovery in this case' is inadequate.
Further responses shall be made within 15 days of notice of this order. Order was signed in open court. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Disclosure Of Information Pertaining To Pltf'S Mental/Emotional Condition And Treatment
Off Calendar Jan-12-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Jenny Li, Esq., for defendant and moving party: John S. Dupen
William S. Weisberg, Esq., for Plaintiff and Opposing party: Shahrzad Saeed

HEARING HELD AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: Defendant John S. Dupen's Motion To Compel Disclosure Of Information Pertaining To Plaintiff's Mental/Emotional Condition And Treatment is granted as it is sought by the testimony cited in the motion. The stipulation offered pursuant to C.C.P. Section 2032.320 goes solely to the issue of a mental examination. The order makes no determination of the appropriateness of any discovery of pre-existing conditions or treatment. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED
